clauseTitle: Understanding the Organisation and Its Context
requirement: Determine external and internal issues relevant to the organisation's purpose that affect the AI management system's ability to achieve its intended outcomes, including AI-specific considerations such as AI technology landscape, ethical expectations, and regulatory environment.

clauseTitle: Understanding the Needs and Expectations of Interested Parties
requirement: Identify interested parties relevant to the AIMS, their requirements related to AI systems, and which requirements will be addressed through the management system.

clauseTitle: Determining the Scope of the AI Management System
requirement: Define the boundaries and applicability of the AIMS, considering internal and external issues, interested party requirements, and the AI systems within scope.
Establish, implement, maintain, and continually improve the AIMS including the processes needed and their interactions, in accordance with the requirements of this standard.

Top management shall demonstrate leadership and commitment with respect to the AIMS by ensuring policy and objectives are established, resources are available, and the importance of effective AI management is communicated.
Establish an AI policy appropriate to the purpose of the organisation that provides a framework for setting AI objectives and includes commitments to responsible AI development and use.
Assign and communicate responsibilities and authorities for relevant roles within the AIMS, including AI governance roles, system owners, and oversight functions.

clauseTitle: Actions to Address Risks and Opportunities
requirement: Determine risks and opportunities related to AI that need to be addressed to ensure the AIMS achieves its intended outcomes, prevents or reduces undesired effects, and achieves continual improvement.

clauseTitle: AI Objectives and Planning to Achieve Them
requirement: Establish AI objectives at relevant functions, levels and processes, ensuring they are consistent with the AI policy, measurable, monitored, communicated, and updated as appropriate.
Determine and provide the resources needed for the establishment, implementation, maintenance and continual improvement of the AIMS, including human resources with AI competence.
Determine necessary competence of persons doing work under the organisation's control that affects AI management performance, ensure they are competent through education, training or experience.
Ensure persons doing work under the organisation's control are aware of the AI policy, their contribution to AIMS effectiveness, implications of non-conformance, and the ethical implications of AI.
Determine internal and external communications relevant to the AIMS including what to communicate, when, with whom, how, and who communicates, including AI system transparency requirements.
Maintain documented information required by the standard and determined necessary for AIMS effectiveness, including AI system documentation, risk assessments, and impact assessments.
Plan, implement and control the processes needed to meet AIMS requirements and implement risk treatment actions, including criteria for AI system development and deployment.
Perform AI risk assessments at planned intervals or when significant changes are proposed or occur, covering risks to individuals, groups, organisations, and society from AI systems.
Define and apply an AI risk treatment process including selecting appropriate treatment options, determining controls, producing a statement of applicability, and formulating a risk treatment plan.
Assess the potential impacts of AI systems on individuals, groups, and society, considering human rights, fairness, transparency, accountability, safety, and environmental impacts.
Determine what needs to be monitored and measured for AI systems and the AIMS, including performance metrics, bias monitoring, and compliance indicators.
Conduct internal audits at planned intervals to provide information on whether the AIMS conforms to requirements and is effectively implemented and maintained.
Top management shall review the AIMS at planned intervals to ensure its continuing suitability, adequacy and effectiveness, including AI-specific review inputs.
Continually improve the suitability, adequacy and effectiveness of the AIMS, incorporating lessons learned from AI system operations and incidents.
React to nonconformities including AI system failures and incidents, evaluate the need for action to eliminate causes, implement corrective action, and review effectiveness.
